title: Former Sears Building to be site of state's first workforce innovation hub
summary: Governor Hochul announced selection of the former Sears Building at 1300 South Salina Street as New York State's first ON-RAMP Workforce Innovation Hub, a $200M state initiative to connect skilled workers with manufacturing careers. The City of Syracuse and CenterState CEO are leading broader revitalization efforts, with $8.5M in Empire State Development funding approved for the flagship center. Initial training will focus on career exploration, ESOL, financial empowerment, and construction/manufacturing skills.

political_signal.description: State investment aligns with Syracuse Surge inclusive growth strategy; positions Syracuse as flagship hub for regional advanced manufacturing workforce development.

processing_notes: Dollar amount for ON-RAMP program is $200M (not $200 as extracted); specific allocation to Syracuse is $8.5M
Key stakeholder alignment: Governor (state), Mayor Walsh (city), CenterState CEO (convener)
Temporary operations at 115 West Fayette St. during South Salina redevelopment
Three additional hubs planned for Capital Region, Mohawk Valley, Finger Lakes
Historic preservation angle: adaptive reuse of former Sears Building
Related city initiative: Syracuse Surge strategy mentioned
